steps.description: Note the coefficient of x is negative.
steps.explanation: When we divide (or multiply) an inequality by a negative number, the inequality sign must be reversed — the key idea here.
We divide both sides by $-3$ to isolate $x$, remembering the sign will flip.
Because we divided by a negative number, $>$ becomes $<$, giving $x < -4$.
Any value of $x$ less than $-4$ satisfies the inequality.
Substituting $x = -5$ (which is less than $-4$) gives $15 > 12$, confirming the solution.

steps.description: Check a value on the wrong side.
steps.explanation: Testing $x = 0$ gives $0 > 12$, which is false, confirming that values above $-4$ do not work.

steps.workingLatex: \text{forgetting to flip would give the wrong side}
steps.explanation: Had we kept $>$, we would have written $x > -4$, the exact opposite set — so the flip is essential.
In set notation the solution is the set of all real $x$ that are less than $-4$.
